commit.message:
fix(art): add missing endblock tag — fixes 500 on /art route

art.html had 2 {% block %} opens (content + scripts) but only 1 {% endblock %},
causing Jinja2 TemplateSyntaxError on every /art request.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- make_ohlcv_with_volume (volume tests, custom volume values)

Harmonize feature name pattern: all feature modules now use inline
string literals in @register_feature() and pd.Series name=, consistent
with log_returns.py, volume.py, and volatility.py which use inline or
dynamic names.

* [RC-0005] FIX M-4: document configurable IQR vs traditional Q25-Q75

Clarify in RobustScaler docstring that 'IQR' refers to the configurable
inter-quantile range (default 0.5%-99.5%), not the traditional Q25-Q75,
as spec §9.2 is ambiguous on this point.

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

>

> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Fix: Build failure in fuzz_test.go due to missing
ProxyOptions argument</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> The recently merged Pull Request for Fuzz testing was created before
the "Functional Options" PR was merged.
> The Functional Options update changed the signatures of internal
functions like `scanAcceptHeader` and `check` to accept a
`*ProxyOptions` pointer as their second argument. Because `fuzz_test.go`
was not updated to reflect these new signatures during the merge, the CI
build is now failing with the following errors:
> 
> ```text
> Error: ./fuzz_test.go:52:24: not enough arguments in call to
scanAcceptHeader
> 	have (*http.Response)
> 	want (*http.Response, *ProxyOptions)
> Error: ./fuzz_test.go:92:13: not enough arguments in call to check
> 	have (*http.Response)
> 	want (*http.Response, *ProxyOptions)
> ```
> 
> ### Proposal
> Update `fuzz_test.go` to pass a valid `*ProxyOptions` instance to the
`scanAcceptHeader` and `check` functions.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - Edit `fuzz_test.go`.
> - Create a default `ProxyOptions` struct (e.g., `opts :=
&ProxyOptions{}`) or configure it based on the fuzzing scenario.
> - Pass `opts` as the second argument to `scanAcceptHeader` and
`check`.
> 
> ### Example Fix
> ```go
> // In fuzz_test.go
> 
> // Create a default options instance for the test
> opts := &ProxyOptions{
> EnableAVIF: false, // or true, depending on what the fuzz test expects
> }
> 
> // Update the function calls
> // Before: format := scanAcceptHeader(resp)
> // After:
> format := scanAcceptHeader(resp, opts)
> 
> // Before: typ := check(resp)
> // After:
> typ := check(resp, opts)
> ```</issue_description>

Replace hardcoded Thread.Sleep/Task.Delay with polling loops in tests (#965)

Replace timing-dependent Thread.Sleep and Task.Delay calls with
Stopwatch-based polling loops across 5 test files. This eliminates
flaky failures under CI load by waiting for conditions to be met
rather than assuming fixed delays are sufficient.

- BackgroundJobServiceTests: poll for job status transitions
- ClientRequestTrackerTests: poll Snapshot() for block expiry
  (avoids ShouldThrottle which increments counters)
- OutputCacheTests: poll TryGet for cache expiry
- AggregateLockManagerTests: poll TryAcquire for lock expiry
- DiskBufferedLoggerTests: poll for log file existence

commit.message:
fix: fix slides after #show/#set rules not rendering subsequent slides (#268)

* Initial plan

* fix: correctly handle styled nodes containing subsequent slide headings

When a `#show` or `#set` rule creates a `styled` node whose inner content
contains headings that break into new slides, the rendered new slides were
previously wrapped in `_delayed-wrapper`. This wrapper only shows content on
the last subslide, so when the Second Slide had only one subslide, the Third
Slide's content was never rendered.

The fix: when the recursive `split-content-into-slides` call on a styled
node's inner content returns non-none `slide-content-part` (meaning new
slides were found), add the pre-heading content to the current slide, flush
the current slide, then push the new slides directly to `output-slides`
instead of wrapping them in `_delayed-wrapper`.

When no new slides are found (`slide-content-part == none`), the original
`_delayed-wrapper` behaviour is preserved for correct subslide animations.

Fixes: slides after a table with `#show table.cell: it => {}` not rendering

commit.message:
Add per-bone is_kinematic flag to pipeline design

Kinematic bones skip Euler-Verlet integration entirely — they are
purely animation-driven (inv_mass=0, no external forces). Dynamic

commit.message:
feat(skill-eval): deterministic + LLM-as-judge eval harness for SF skills

SOURCE: philschmid.de/testing-skills (public article)
WHY: SF had 60+ skills shipped without systematic evals — the "vibe-checked
with a handful of manual runs, then shipped" anti-pattern. SkillsBench
(arxiv:2602.12670) counted 47k+ skills across GitHub with near-zero eval
coverage. We port the article's full harness approach.

## platform/tools/skill_eval_tools.py (eval engine)
- Deterministic checks: regex/not_regex/length_min/has_keyword/has_section/no_placeholder
  (fast, zero LLM cost — article: "deterministic checks are fast")
- LLM-as-judge: structured JSON output for qualitative expectations
  (article: "use structured output to constrain the LLM's response to a typed schema")
- Multi-trial: N runs per case, report distribution (article: "run 3-5 trials")
- 3 dimensions: outcome (works?), style (conventions?), efficiency (tokens)
- Negative tests: should_trigger:false cases prevent over-broad skill descriptions
- Retirement detection: if skill passes without being loaded → retire it
- Pass rate ≥80% = ready to ship (article graduation rule)
- Results persisted to data/skill_evals/<skill>.json

## platform/web/routes/api/skill_eval.py (REST API)
- GET /api/skills/eval — coverage summary (total, %, passing, gaps)
- GET /api/skills/eval/{name} — full last result per skill
- POST /api/skills/eval/{name}/run — async trigger with job_id
- GET /api/skills/eval/job/{job_id} — poll job status
- GET /api/skills/list — all skills with eval coverage metadata

## /art — 5th tab "Skills Health"
- Coverage progress bar (% of skills with eval_cases)
- Table: skills sorted by pass rate (green ≥80%, yellow ≥60%, red <60%)
- Panel: skills missing eval_cases (named list)
- Graduation tip + link to skill-eval workflow

## sf CLI: sf skills eval + sf skills coverage
- sf skills eval <name> — run eval harness, show per-case breakdown
- sf skills eval --all — run all skills with eval_cases
- sf skills eval <name> --trials=5 — override trial count
- sf skills coverage — quick coverage snapshot

## eval_cases added to 4 skills (3 cases each, with checks + expectations)
- skills/tdd.md — tdd-new-feature, tdd-bug-regression, tdd-no-trigger-doc
- skills/code-review.md — review-with-bug (SQL injection), clean-code, perf
- skills/error-monitoring.md — classify-new, classify-regression, ongoing-suppress
- skills/clean-code.md — no-stub-allowed, no-mock-data, edge-case-handled
Coverage: 10/57 skills (18%) → foundation for iterating to 100%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: change ov_bitarray from size_t to uint8_t

Eliminates endianness issues when casting bitarray pointers to
uint8_t* for base64 serialization. The previous size_t-based
implementation produced different byte sequences on different
platforms when raw memory was serialized.

Changes:
- typedef: size_t -> uint8_t
- Simplified set/get/clear inline functions (no more word/bit split)
- length_to_bytes: now just (bits + 7) / 8
- OV_ARRAY_HEADER macro: cast through void* to suppress -Wcast-align
  warning for uint8_t* arrays (actual alignment is guaranteed by allocator)

* fix: prevent infinite reload loop on empty cognitive map/decision data

Add decisionsLoaded and mapLoaded guard flags to prevent reactive
statements from re-triggering loadCognitiveMap/loadDecisions when
the backend returns empty results. Previously, the condition
mapNodes.length === 0 && !mapLoading would immediately re-trigger
the fetch after it completed with zero nodes.

* fix: clear stale data on cognitive map refresh

Reset mapNodes and mapEdges arrays when refresh button is clicked
to prevent stale data from lingering if the re-fetch fails or
returns empty results.

- ReasoningSessionViewer called non-existent /api/transparency/sessions/active (404)

commit.message:
feat: in-memory columnar store with SIMD query path (fixes #909) (#959)

- ColumnarStore: maintains dense typed arrays (int[], long[], double[], float[])
  per numeric field, built lazily from loaded objects, invalidated on Save/Delete
- SIMD column scans via Vector<T> on pre-built arrays — no per-query object
  loading or property extraction for numeric predicates
- WalDataProvider integration: columnar path activates for 256+ row entities
  when all query clauses target numeric fields; only matching rows are loaded
- Invalidation on Save, Delete, and SaveRecord ensures consistency
- 9 new tests: column population, scan operators (Equals, GreaterThan,
  LessThan, NotEquals), multi-clause AND, key mapping, version invalidation
